audit_log.check: Autonomy vs Automation: PASS (frameworks guide visual strategy, not cookie-cutter templates)
Three-Layer Architecture: PASS (niche strategy → style frameworks → AI production execution)
Consciousness Awareness: PASS (maps to SPECIAL-LEFT creative expression + SMART-RIGHT systematic production)
Quality Standards: PASS (native formats prioritized over polished commercial content)
No Manipulation: PASS (authentic visuals, no deceptive "before/after" for sensitive categories)
Proof Discipline: PASS (all visual strategies source-tagged to expert training)
Platform Compliance: PASS (Meta image requirements, prohibited content avoided)

dependency.integration_points: Creative concepts from Andromeda Engine executed as visual assets by this module
dependency.notes: Lazy Static Headlines, Scam Hooks, Founder VSLs require specific visual execution covered here

dependency.integration_points: Generated images analyzed for hook rate and visual effectiveness

consciousness_profile.description: Provides niche-specific visual frameworks and AI-accelerated production workflows for generating high-performance static ad images at scale. Focuses on native formats, text-overlay strategies, and rapid iteration using GenAI tools.

component.activation_strategy: LEFT creative visual expression (Ecom product beauty vs SaaS dashboard simplicity) + RIGHT logical niche-matching (health needs testimonials, B2B needs authority)
component.consciousness_purpose: Designers understand both creative freedom (LEFT_SPECIAL) and strategic constraints (RIGHT_SMART: "Not all visuals work for all niches")

balance_rules.rule: RIGHT_SMART niche analysis before LEFT_SPECIAL creative execution
Each niche has proven visual patterns: Ecom (lifestyle), SaaS (dashboard), Health (testimonial), Lead Gen (direct call-out)
Testing validates which niche strategy resonates (RIGHT_SMART data)

component.activation_strategy: RIGHT systematic AI workflows (Nano Banana, Midjourney batch generation) + BOTTOM risk mitigation (test cheap statics before expensive video production)
component.consciousness_purpose: Producers scale creative volume efficiently (RIGHT_SMART) while protecting budget (BOTTOM_SAFE: "Don't overproduce losers")

balance_rules.rule: BOTTOM_SAFE test-first: Static + text overlay validation before full photoshoot
RIGHT_SMART workflows: Nano Banana generates 10 variants in 10 minutes vs 10 hours manual design
Rule of 1:10k requires RIGHT_SMART systematic production to meet volume demands

component.activation_strategy: LEFT creative pattern interrupt (Notes App, Breaking News formats) + TOP compelling action (native visuals drive higher CTR than polished ads)
component.consciousness_purpose: Designers embrace "ugly" over "beautiful" (LEFT_SPECIAL unconventional aesthetics) to achieve performance (TOP_STRONG results)

balance_rules.rule: LEFT_SPECIAL embraces imperfection: UGC-style, iPhone screenshots, handwritten text outperform studio quality
TOP_STRONG validates approach: Native formats achieve 20-40% higher CTR
If client demands "on-brand" polish → Split test to prove data (RIGHT_SMART)

core_principle.name: Visuals as Targeting Mechanism
core_principle.principle: The image itself selects the audience through visual cues and composition. "Ugly" or "Native" visuals often outperform polished/branded content because they bypass ad blindness and appear as organic content in the feed.

key_insights.insight: Native formats (Notes App, News Headers, UGC) outperform commercial studio content [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 11]
Indirect visuals (curiosity-driven, no product) scale cold traffic better than direct product shots [SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 843]
Direct visuals (product + offer) work best for retargeting warm audiences [SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 845]
Text overlay density matters: <20% of image should be text (Meta preference) [SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 846]
Square format (1:1, 1080x1080) is default; vertical (9:16) essential for Reels placement [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 19]

production_priority_order.priority_1: Volume over perfection: 10 native-style statics > 1 polished studio shot
production_priority_order.priority_2: Test statics first: Validate concept with text overlay before video production
production_priority_order.priority_3: AI-accelerate winners: Use Nano Banana to generate 10 variants of winning static
production_priority_order.priority_4: Native format default: Start with UGC-style, then test polished if client demands

framework.name: Ecom Product Visuals
framework.summary: Visual strategies for ecom DTC products focusing on lifestyle context, "Lazy Static" text overlays, and rapid variant generation.

strategy.name: Lifestyle Context
strategy.description: Product shown in use (person wearing clothing, supplement on kitchen counter, skincare in bathroom)
strategy.why_it_works: Aspirational; shows product in real-world context vs sterile white background
strategy.production: Can use AI (Midjourney/Nano Banana) to place product in lifestyle scenes

strategy.name: Lazy Static + Text Overlay
strategy.description: Clean product shot with bold text overlay using Lazy Static formulas (e.g., "Knee Pain Gone")
strategy.why_it_works: Fast to produce; clearly communicates benefit; easy to test 10+ variants
strategy.reference: See KB-ANDROMEDA-CREATIVE-ENGINE Framework F2 for 11 headline formulas

strategy.name: Timeline Progression
strategy.description: 3-panel split showing "24 Hours / 3 Days / 3 Weeks" results
strategy.why_it_works: Increases AOV by encouraging bulk purchases for long-term transformation
strategy.example: "Day 1: Pain Relief | Day 3: Mobility | Day 21: Full Recovery"

strategy.name: Color Blocking
strategy.description: Solid color background (black, yellow, red) with high-contrast product image
strategy.why_it_works: Stands out in white Instagram/Facebook feed via involuntary eye attraction

ai_production_workflow.step: Start with 1 high-quality product photo (flat lay or lifestyle)
Upload to Nano Banana (Gemini): "Place this product in [kitchen/gym/office] scene"
Apply Lazy Static formulas to each variant in Canva (10 text overlays × 5 images = 50 variants)
Export top 10-15 for testing in Pack System

quality_gates.gate: Product must be clearly visible (not obscured by text or clutter)
Text overlay <20% of image area (Meta preference)
Mobile-first: Preview on phone before export (80% of views are mobile)

framework.name: SaaS Dashboard Visuals
framework.source_tag: [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 50, Mark Builds Brands | 823]
framework.summary: Visual strategies for SaaS and software products emphasizing dashboard screenshots, data visualization, and B2B authority positioning.

strategy.name: Dashboard Demo Screenshot
strategy.description: Clean screenshot of software UI showing key features or data dashboard
strategy.why_it_works: Product proof; shows what user will actually see; builds trust through transparency
strategy.production_tips: Blur sensitive data; highlight key feature with red box or arrow; use fake data that looks real

strategy.name: Hormozi 'Calling Card'
strategy.description: White background, black text, direct avatar call-out: "If you're a [specific persona]..."
strategy.why_it_works: Extreme minimalism; self-selection; cuts through noise for B2B buyers
strategy.example: "If you're a 7-figure landscaping business, we want to help you scale"

strategy.name: Data Visualization
strategy.description: Chart or graph showing results (upward trend line, before/after metrics)
strategy.why_it_works: Logical proof for analytical B2B buyers; shows ROI potential

strategy.name: Feature Comparison Table
strategy.description: Side-by-side comparison: "Us vs Them" or "Before vs After"
strategy.why_it_works: Competitive differentiation; highlights unique value props clearly
strategy.layout: Left column: Competitor (red X marks), Right column: Your product (green checkmarks)

production_workflow.step: Take clean dashboard screenshot (use demo account with realistic data)
Edit in Figma/Photoshop: Blur sensitive fields, highlight 1-2 key features
Add text overlay (optional): "[Feature Name]: 10X Faster Than [Competitor]"
Export in 1:1 and 9:16 for feed + Reels placement

quality_gates.gate: Screenshot must be legible on mobile (text size >14pt)
Avoid cluttered UI; show 1-2 features max per image
Use realistic demo data (not "Lorem Ipsum" or obviously fake numbers)

framework.name: Lead Gen Direct Call-Out
framework.summary: Visual strategies for lead generation and service businesses using direct text-based call-outs to qualify prospects immediately.

strategy.name: Text-Heavy Direct Call-Out
strategy.description: Minimal background, maximum text: "If you're [specific persona] and struggling with [pain], we can help"
strategy.why_it_works: Immediate self-selection; filters low-intent leads; B2B buyers read text ads
strategy.example: "If you're a contractor doing $2M+ revenue and can't find good labor..."

strategy.name: Authority Positioning
strategy.description: Founder headshot + credentials overlaid (certifications, years in business, results)
strategy.why_it_works: Trust-building for high-ticket services; human connection
strategy.example: Founder photo + text: "20 Years Litigation Experience | 150+ Cases Won"

strategy.name: Case Study Preview
strategy.description: Before/After metrics with client logo (if permitted)
strategy.why_it_works: Social proof + concrete results = high-quality lead filtering
strategy.example: "Case Study: How We Helped [Company] Reduce Legal Spend 40%"

strategy.name: Demographic Call-Out
strategy.description: Image or text specifically naming target demographic: "Over 50?" or "Parents of Teens?"
strategy.why_it_works: Visual self-selection; Andromeda serves ad to called-out demographic

production_workflow.step: Define ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) with specificity: "Ecom brands doing $500k-2M revenue"
Write direct call-out copy targeting that ICP
Design in Canva: White or black background, large sans-serif font, minimal graphics
Optional: Add founder photo or logo for brand recognition
Test 5-10 variations with different pain points or demographics

framework.name: Health/Wellness Testimonial Visuals
framework.summary: Visual strategies for health, wellness, and supplement products using testimonial-driven formats and native app interfaces.

strategy.name: Notes App Testimonial
strategy.description: Screenshot of iPhone Notes app with testimonial text and small product image embedded
strategy.why_it_works: Feels personal and authentic; bypasses ad blindness by looking like organic content
strategy.example: Yellow Notes background: "What finally helped my eczema... [bullet points] [product image]"

strategy.name: Breaking News Format
strategy.description: Image formatted like news article or blog with header "Breaking News" or "Now Available to Public"
strategy.why_it_works: Authority positioning; curiosity-driven; looks informational not commercial
strategy.example: Red banner "Breaking News" + product image + headline "The Supplement Doctors Are Recommending"

strategy.name: Layered Social Proof
strategy.description: Combining multiple proof types: Doctor recommendation + star rating + customer count + science mention
strategy.why_it_works: Overcomes skepticism through proof stacking
strategy.elements: Doctor photo OR white coat image + "4.8★ from 12,000 customers" + "Clinically Studied"

strategy.name: Transformation Grid
strategy.description: Before/After grid OR timeline progression with customer photos (if compliant)
strategy.why_it_works: Visual proof of transformation; high engagement
strategy.compliance_note: For sensitive categories (weight loss, skin conditions), ensure Meta Ad Policy compliance; avoid exaggerated claims

production_workflow.step: Collect real customer testimonials (reviews, emails, social media posts)
For Notes App format: Screenshot Notes app template, paste testimonial text, embed product image
For Breaking News: Use news article template (Canva), add red "Breaking News" banner, insert product + headline
Generate 5-10 variants with different testimonials or headlines

compliance_guardrails.guardrail: No exaggerated "before/after" claims for weight loss, skin conditions, or medical issues
Use real testimonials; do not fabricate customer quotes
Avoid words like "cure," "miracle," "guaranteed results" (Meta policy violations)

framework.name: Info Product Authority Visuals
framework.summary: Visual strategies for courses, coaching, and info products emphasizing founder authority and educational value.

strategy.name: Founder Letter Static
strategy.description: Black background, white text, formatted like personal letter from founder with product image in corner
strategy.why_it_works: Personal connection; founder transparency builds trust for high-ticket offers
strategy.example: "In 3 years, I've never done this... [offer details]... - Charlie, Founder" + brand logo

strategy.name: Whiteboard Lesson Screenshot
strategy.description: Screenshot from educational video showing whiteboard or diagram with key concept
strategy.why_it_works: Demonstrates expertise; gives free value upfront; positions founder as teacher
strategy.example: Whiteboard with diagram + text overlay: "The 3-Step Framework I Use With $10M Clients"

strategy.name: Results Screenshot
strategy.description: Screenshot of student/client results: revenue dashboards, testimonials, success metrics
strategy.why_it_works: Social proof via student success; "proof in the pudding"
strategy.example: Stripe dashboard screenshot showing "$127k in 30 days" + blur personal info

strategy.name: Behind-the-Scenes
strategy.description: Founder in workspace, filming setup, or "making of" style image
strategy.why_it_works: Authenticity; humanizes brand; builds parasocial connection

production_workflow.step: Screenshot best frame (founder speaking, good lighting)
Add text overlay: Key quote or value prop
Alternative: Use Canva to create Founder Letter format with product image

framework.name: Lazy Static System
framework.reference: See KB-ANDROMEDA-CREATIVE-ENGINE Framework F2 for complete details
framework.summary: 11 text-overlay formulas requiring zero design skill: "Pain Point Gone," "Timeline Ads," "No More [X]," etc.
framework.application: Apply to any niche; works for ecom, health, productivity products

framework.name: Nano Banana AI Swap
framework.summary: Using Gemini (Nano Banana Pro) to generate infinite variations by swapping models, backgrounds, or products while maintaining composition.

workflow.step: Upload winning static ad to Gemini chat
Upload product image or desired background scene
Prompt: "Replace [element A] in image 1 with [element B] from image 2. Keep pose/composition identical."
Export and test in Pack System

examples.example: Swap 1: "Replace the woman in image 1 with a man of similar age"
Swap 2: "Change background from kitchen to outdoor park"
Swap 3: "Replace product in image 1 with the bottle from image 2"
Swap 4: "Make it nighttime instead of daytime, keep everything else identical"

use_cases.use_case: Scaling winning static to combat fatigue (extend lifespan 2-4 weeks)
Testing demographic variations (age, gender, ethnicity) to find new audience pockets
A/B testing backgrounds (indoor vs outdoor, minimal vs busy)

framework.name: Visual Chaos Reduction
framework.principle: Remove distractions to create single focal point, ensuring eye goes exactly where intended (product, face, or "weird" texture).

steps.step: Identify intended focal point (what should viewer look at first?)
Blur or remove background distractions using AI background removal or Photoshop
Ensure high contrast between subject and background (dark subject on light bg or vice versa)
Squint test: Can you identify focal point with eyes half-closed? If yes, it's clear enough

common_fixes.fix: Cluttered background → AI background removal, replace with solid color or simple gradient
Multiple subjects → Crop tighter on primary subject, blur secondary elements
Low contrast → Increase saturation on subject, desaturate background

framework.name: Timeline Ad Structure
framework.summary: 3-column split layout showing progression of results over time to increase Average Order Value.
framework.why_it_works: Encourages bulk buying ("I need 3 weeks supply to see permanent results") → Higher AOV

production.template: Create 3-column template (1080x1080 square divided into thirds)
production.step: Add product image at top or bottom spanning all columns
Add timeline text in each column with clear visual separation (borders or bg colors)

framework.name: Us vs Them Comparison
framework.summary: Split-screen static comparing your product (Hero) to competitor or industry standard (Villain).

left_side.label: Them (or "Old Way" / "Competitor")
left_side.visual_treatment: Black and white or desaturated filter

applications.application: Traditional marketing agency vs AI-powered solution
Old skincare routine (5 steps) vs streamlined product (1 step)

sop.name: Nano Banana Production Sprint
sop.purpose: Rapidly generate 10-15 static variants using AI to extend lifespan of winning concept and combat fatigue.

steps.step: Identify top 3 performing static ads from last 14 days (filter by: Static Image, CPA $1k)
Source 3 competitor templates using MagicBrief or Meta Ads Library (ads running >14 days)
Open Gemini (Nano Banana Pro model)
Prompt Set 1: "Swap background of [Winner A] with [Lifestyle Setting]" → Generate 5 versions
Prompt Set 2: "Put my product [Upload Image] into competitor template [Upload]" → Generate 5 versions
Prompt Set 3: "Change model in [Winner A] to [Different Demographic]" → Generate 5 versions
Export 15 new variants (total production time: ~45 minutes)
Upload to Prospecting CBO as new Pack: "Pack [Date] - Nano Banana Variants"

quality_gates.gate: AI-generated images must be photorealistic (no obvious artifacts or distortions)
Brand consistency: Logo placement and product representation accurate
Mobile preview: Check all variants on phone before export

sop.expected_output: 15 static variants ready for testing in 45-60 minutes

sop.name: Lazy Static Batch Creation
sop.purpose: Produce 10+ static variants quickly using text-overlay formulas for high-volume testing.

steps.step: Select 1 high-quality product photo (lifestyle shot or clean product on white background)
Open Canva, create 1080x1080 canvas
Upload product image, duplicate canvas 11 times
Apply all 11 Lazy Static formulas as text overlays (see Andromeda Creative Engine F2 for formulas)
Font selection: Bold, sans-serif, high contrast (white text on dark image or black text on light image)
Export all 11 variants as PNG (1080x1080)
Upload to testing campaign

sop.name: Competitor Swipe & Rewrite
sop.purpose: Identify winning competitor ad creative and recreate with your brand/product.

steps.step: Find 5-star performing ad on Meta Ads Library or "Get Hooked" tool (filter: active >7 days)
Screenshot competitor ad image
Upload to Gemini or Claude AI with prompt: "Analyze why this ad works. Recreate the layout but use my brand colors [Hex Code] and my product [Upload Image]. Write new copy for [My Niche]."
Take AI prompt output and execute in Midjourney or Canva
Generate 3-5 variants with different headlines or product angles
Test in Pack System

sop.ethical_note: Recreate layout/structure, not exact copy; ensure no copyright infringement

sop.name: Seasonal Banner Injection
sop.purpose: Refresh winning evergreen ads with seasonal offers without expensive re-shoots.

steps.step: Download top 5 evergreen winning ads (Static or Video thumbnail)
Do NOT re-design from scratch; overlay simple banner only
Design banner in Canva: "Black Friday Sale is Live" or "30% OFF Ends Tonight"
For video: Add banner to first 3 seconds (hook) using video editor
For static: Overlay banner at top or bottom (20% of image max)
Launch as new ads alongside evergreen versions

sop.key_principle: Leverage existing winners; don't start from scratch for seasonal campaigns

sop.name: Visual Hook Audit (Pre-Launch)
sop.purpose: Validate that static images have clear focal points and will stop scroll.

audit_checklist.check: Squint Test: Can you identify focal point immediately with eyes half-closed?
Visual Chaos: Is there clutter in background? (If yes → blur or remove)
Curiosity Trigger: Does it create question in viewer's mind? ("What is that weird rock?")
Text Readability: Can text be read in <1 second on mobile? (Test on phone)
Contrast Check: Is subject high-contrast against background?

sop.pass_criteria: If image passes 4/5 checks → Launch. If <3/5 → Redesign before launch

category.name: Diagnostic
category.heuristic: IF static ads have high CPM THEN reduce text density (Meta prefers visual over text-heavy images) [SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 846]
IF CTR is low on product ad THEN switch to "Indirect" curiosity image (weird texture/outcome) to widen net [SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 843]
IF targeting skeptical audience THEN use "Scam Hook" visual ("I thought this was a scam...") to leverage loss aversion [SRC: Dara Denney | 496]
IF hook rate is high but hold rate is low THEN visual hook is clickbait and doesn't match payoff [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 483]
IF in "boring" niche (insurance, B2B) THEN use "Hormozi Call-Out" (black/white text) to qualify immediately [SRC: Dr. Matt Shiver | 50]

category.name: Optimization
category.heuristic: IF a static ad works THEN use AI (Nano Banana) to swap model's ethnicity or gender to find new audience pockets [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 21]
IF writing headlines for statics THEN use "Pain Point + Gone" for instant clarity [SRC: Tiana Asperjan | 25]
IF you have a winning angle THEN create a "Native News" version to build authority [SRC: Franky Shaw | 235]
IF selling a supplement THEN use "Timeline Ad" format to encourage higher AOV (bulk buys) [SRC: Tiana Asperjan | 30]
IF want to stop scroll THEN use "Color Blocking" (all black image on white feed) [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 833]

category.name: Scaling
category.heuristic: IF you spend $10k/month THEN produce at least 1 new creative concept per week (Rule of 1:10k) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 190]
IF you find a winning "Concept" (e.g., UGC in car) THEN create 10 variations of that specific concept [SRC: Sam Piliero | 194]
IF scaling a winner THEN do not edit the live ad; duplicate Post ID to scaling campaign [SRC: Professor Charley T | 103]
IF need volume THEN use "Creative Formatting" (same image, different crop/layout) to trick Andromeda into seeing it as new [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 19]

category.name: Troubleshooting
category.heuristic: IF ads look too "Ad-like" THEN use "Notes App" style to blend natively with user content [SRC: Omar Eddaoudi | 268]
IF can't film new content THEN use "Stock Footage + Text Overlay" (Storyblocks) to create conversational hooks [SRC: Dara Denney | 500]
IF customers are price-sensitive THEN use "Investment Hooks" (showing time/money wasted on other solutions) [SRC: Dara Denney | 494]
IF need to build trust fast THEN use a "Founder Ad" explaining the why behind the brand [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 449]

example.name: The Weird Rock Gum Ad
example.visual_description: Hand holding what looks like brown, textured "rocks" (actually natural gum resin)
example.why_it_works: Looks like fecal matter or something gross; creates instant curiosity ("What is that?")

example.name: The Lululemon AI Swap
example.visual_description: Original: Man in t-shirt. Variant: Exact same man, same pose, wearing windbreaker jacket
example.production_method: Nano Banana (Gemini): "Replace t-shirt in image 1 with windbreaker from image 2, keep pose identical"
example.why_it_works: Tests product demand without new photoshoot; maintains proven composition

example.name: The Breaking News Knife Ad
example.visual_description: Header: "Breaking News" in red banner. Image: Chef (Gordon Ramsay lookalike or AI-generated) holding Damascus blade
example.headline: "The Blade Behind Michelin Star Kitchens"
example.why_it_works: Looks like news discovery, not product push; authority positioning via news format
example.expected_performance: High CTR from curiosity; bypasses ad blindness

example.name: The Eczema Notes App Ad
example.visual_description: Standard iPhone Notes app interface, yellow background. Text list: "Gut + Skin approach," "No steroids," "Itch calmed in days." Small product bottle photo at bottom
example.why_it_works: Feels native, personal, authentic; looks like friend's recommendation not ad
example.production_time: 15 minutes (screenshot Notes template, add text + product image)

knowledge_block.metrics_table: <metric name="CTR (Click-Through Rate)" purpose="Interest signal for static ads" threshold=">1.5% (static), >2% (video)" action_trigger="IF <1.0% → Test new visuals or hooks" source="[SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 846]"/> <metric name="Hook Rate (3s Video Plays / Impressions)" purpose="Visual attention capture" threshold=">30%" action_trigger="IF <25% → Audit visual focal point" source="[SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 849]"/> <metric name="Text Overlay Density" purpose="Meta platform preference" threshold="<20% of image area" action_trigger="IF >20% → May face delivery issues or higher CPM" source="[SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 846]"/>

patterns.pattern: Native formats (UGC, Notes App, News) outperform polished studio content by 20-40% CTR [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 11]

pattern.name: Indirect for Cold, Direct for Warm

patterns.pattern: Curiosity visuals (no product shown) for Prospecting; product + offer for Retargeting [SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 845]
1:1 (1080x1080) works everywhere; 9:16 required for Reels/Stories placement [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 19]
Use Nano Banana to generate 10 variants of winning static in 10 minutes [SRC: Sam Piliero | 610]
Lazy Static formulas produce 11 variants in 60 minutes [SRC: Tiana Asperjan | 25]

checklist.name: Pre-Launch Static Batch Validation
checklist.item: [ ] Concept Diversity: Are these 3+ distinct concepts, not variations? [SRC: Alex Robinson | 121]
[ ] Visual Hook Audit: Clear focal point in each image? [SRC: Fraser Cottrell | 830]
[ ] Mobile Preview: Checked all images on phone? (80% of views are mobile) [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 19]
[ ] Text Readability: Can text be read in <1 second? Font size >40pt? [SRC: Chase Chappell | 730]
[ ] Text Density: Is text overlay <20% of image area? [SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 846]
[ ] Format Check: Exported in 1:1 (feed) and 9:16 (Reels)? [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 19]
[ ] Brand Consistency: Logo visible but not overwhelming? [Constitutional Compliance]

checklist.name: Weekly Image Production Review
checklist.item: [ ] Meeting Rule of 1:10k? (1 concept per week per $10k spend) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 190]
[ ] Identified top 3 winning statics from last 14 days? [SRC: Sam Piliero | 610]
[ ] Scheduled Nano Banana sprint to generate variants of winners? [SRC: Sam Piliero | 615]
[ ] Sourced 1 new competitor concept from Ads Library? [SRC: Sam Piliero | 613]
[ ] Created 10+ Lazy Static variants for testing? [SRC: Tiana Asperjan | 25]

compliance_guardrails.guardrail: No exaggerated "before/after" claims for weight loss, skin conditions, or medical issues (Meta policy violation) [Meta Ad Policies]
Use real testimonials; do not fabricate customer quotes or reviews [Constitutional Compliance + Meta Policies]
Text overlay must be <20% of image area for optimal delivery (Meta preference) [SRC: Mark Builds Brands | 846]
All images must be mobile-optimized: preview on phone before export (80% of users on mobile) [SRC: Justin Lalonde | 19]
Avoid words like "cure," "miracle," "guaranteed results" in text overlays (Meta policy red flags) [Meta Ad Policies]
AI-generated images must be photorealistic with no obvious artifacts (quality standard) [SRC: Sam Piliero | 617]
